_descriptor_type: A type of descriptor. One of 'simulation', 'trace', and 'perf'.
_architecture: A name of the available architectures (PARAMS.<architecture>) found in scarab repository, which defines the machine parameters of the default configuration.
_experiment: The name of the experiment. scarab-infra will create a new folder named containing all files needed to execute the simulation in the <ROOT_DIR>/simulations/<experiment> directory.
_workload_manager: manual: run each exp scenario with all the simpoints at a time, slurm: Slurm schedules simulations based on its policy
_root_dir: Root directory mounted into the docker container home, for instance, the user's home directory
_application_dir: The path to the benchmark application directory used for tracing or simulation.
_scarab_path: The path to the scarab repository that contains the scarab binary used to run the experiment.
_traces_dir: The local directory containing all simpointed traces
_top_simpoint: Run only top 3 simpoints stored in 'workloads_top_simp.json'. Must match the attribute-record pass (bld_attr_record) so the <bench>_<simpt>_braddr.csv files cover the simpoints profiled here.
_simulations: Feeder-access profiling pass over SPEC CPU2017 integer (rate_int_v2). Measurement-only: classifies every on-path demand-load access to an attribution feeder line as cold / reuse-hit / reuse-miss. Add other suites/subsuites here to profile them too.
_configurations: Single measurement-only config. Enables branch_load_dep_feeder_access_profile, which counts DCACHE_FEEDER_ACCESS_{TOTAL,COLD,REUSE_HIT,REUSE_MISS} for lines in the attribution feeder set. It does NOT change replacement (no --branch_load_dep_repl) and does NOT force hits (no --branch_load_dep_addr_replay). PREREQUISITE: the per-simpoint <bench>_<simpt>_braddr.csv files from a prior attribute-record pass (bld_attr_record) must exist in --branch_load_dep_trace_dir.

profile._params: Feeder-access stream profiler, coverage 0.9 attribution feeder set.

_mem: Memory scheduling and collection settings for Slurm jobs.
